items.title: Inside the life of the Australian man accused of Thai girl's murder

children.content: Accused murderer Simon Peter Carman spent much of the past decade isolating himself from many of his friends and family in Australia. It's in his rented room in Thailand that local police allege a gruesome murder occurred.

gallery.altText: A graphic compiling a photo of Simon with CCTV stills

thumbnail.altText: A graphic compiling a photo of Simon with CCTV stills

items.title: Statue to be removed after former St Kilda player’s assault conviction

image.altText: Bronze statue of Nicky Winmar at Perth Stadium.

children.content: Former St Kilda and Western Bulldogs player Nicky Winmar has been found guilty of assaulting a woman in Victoria.

items.title: As NAIDOC turns 50, what is 'deadly' and what does it mean to you?

children.content: As official NAIDOC celebrations get underway across the country, the ABC's Matt Francis asks what is deadly and what does it mean to the Indigenous community across the country.

image.altText: a man with blue aviator sunglasses smiling at the camera with a toddler wearing a black shirt with an Aboriginal flag

items.title: Magpie Maynard makes contact with umpire after being hit 'cheaply'

children.content: Collingwood defender Brayden Maynard could be in trouble after making contact with an umpire during the Magpies' six-point win over the Gold Coast Suns.

image.altText: Brayden Maynard of the Magpies is knocked down, holding his ribs

items.title: 'We can't wait': WA town hopeful of economic benefits of reopening gold mine

children.content: The rebirth of the mothballed Badgebup Gold Mine is expected to bring new industry and inject hundreds of workers into one of WA's premier farming regions.

items.title: Suspected bird flu cases detected in NSW and WA after devastating impact overseas

children.content: A suspected positive case of H5 bird flu has been identified in New South Wales, the state agriculture minister has announced.

image.altText: NSW Bennetts Beach where the first suspected case of H5 bird flu was found

items.title: Early morning World Cup viewing parties end in disappointment

children.content: Football fans across the country ride the gamut of emotions as the Socceroos suffer heartbreaking defeat in the FIFA World Cup, having come from behind to force extra time and penalties against Egypt in their knockout clash in Texas.

items.title: 'Similar to a blender': Gina Rinehart defends helipad amid noise concerns

children.content: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart wants to be able to land helicopters on the roof of her corporate HQ, and says the noise experienced by nearby residents would be similar to using a coffee machine or blender.

articles.title: Gina Rinehart and estranged kids to pay large legal costs after royalties trial

children.content: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart and her two estranged children will have to pay a large chunk of the legal costs able to be recovered from a mammoth trial that determined the payment of royalties from some of Australia's most lucrative iron ore mines, a WA judge has ruled.

gallery.altText: Gina Rinehart smiles as she is interviewed by a journalist.

thumbnail.altText: Gina Rinehart smiles as she is interviewed by a journalist.

items.title: Geraldton punk band gains global renown with Jackass movie song

children.content: What began as three mates jamming in a kitchen on Western Australia's mid west coast has turned into playing European festivals to thousands of people and now a song in a new Hollywood film.

image.altText: Two people jump while holding guitars on stage. Another person sings into a microphone.

items.title: Chilling image from January 26 that stops gallery-goers in their tracks

children.content: There is a wide range of artworks among the finalists vying for this year's prestigious National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art Awards, from photography and painting to multimedia and sculpture.

image.altText: A black-and-white photo of a smiling Aboriginal girl on her dad's shoulders as they march through the city.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: 'Grieving best mate' of West Coast Eagles star pens open letter to AFL

children.content: In a five-page letter to the executives and commissioners of the AFL, a grieving best mate has slammed the organisation for "failing" former West Coasts player Adam Hunter, who suffered from CTE.

gallery.altText: Man in AFL singlet holds up trophy

thumbnail.altText: Man in AFL singlet holds up trophy

items.title: When a fit young man disappears, he leaves behind a trail of strange clues

image.altText: kellie stands in front of house holding sign saying "bring jack home"

children.content: When the first major relationship of Jack McLennan's life implodes, he does what plenty of young men do: meets a mate for a quiet beer. But by morning, Jack's disappeared, leaving behind a series of mysterious clues and a devastated family.

items.title: Court hears doctor unknowingly communicated with self-described paedophile vigilante

children.content: A Perth paediatrician alleged to have sexually assaulted child patients was unknowingly communicating online with a self-described paedophile vigilante, a court has heard.

image.altText: A head and shoulders shot of an Asian-Australian man, with black hair, wearing spectacles and a blue and white business shirt.

items.title: What could Taylor Swift's wedding dress look like?

children.content: Singer Taylor Swift has married footballer Travis Kelce, creating a massive pop culture moment and a passionate talking point on social media.

image.altText: A muscular footballer kisses a blonde pop star as media and camera surround them

items.title: Thousands more Perth properties to be subdivided under major shake-up

children.content: Major planning changes will allow more Perth home owners to subdivide their properties in a bid to boost housing supply as the city continues to fall short of its infill target.

gallery.altText: An aerial shot of a suburban streetscape

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of a suburban streetscape

items.title: Prosperous NT mining town prepares for tough times as Rio Tinto exit looms

children.content: In Australia's tropical north, a mining town is preparing for the departure of its biggest employer, and locals are accusing the mining giant of squeezing out small businesses on its way out.

image.altText: Streets of homes and two large green ovals can be seen in an aerial shot of a remote coastal community.

items.title: Trump made money off his meme coin, did its investors?

children.content: US President Donald Trump made huge amounts of money from his family's crypto and meme coin ventures last year, but did his investors?

image.altText: US President Donald Trump speaks at Burning Hills Amphitheatre with his hands outstretched.

items.title: Billionaires to every day people: Inquiry exposes extent of online hate

children.content: Everyone from billionaires to politicians and pro-Palestinian Jewish activists told the royal commission of the hate they receive in phone calls and emails.

image.altText: composite image of Julie Inman Grant, steven lowy and sarah Schwartz

items.title: As WA school holidays loom, fears mount for road safety

children.content: With fatalities on WA roads on track to surpass their highest toll in almost two decades, first responders fear the upcoming school holidays will make things worse.

image.altText: Old school building across the road with two cars moving quickly in front

items.title: If the odds are anything to go by, this 104yo WWII veteran should not be alive

children.content: Eighty five years ago, Brian Walley, a prisoner of war in a German camp after crashing his bomber into the North Sea, doubted he would live for long. Yesterday, he celebrated his 104th birthday.

gallery.altText: An old man sitting in a grey armchair surrounded by his five children, holding his hands.

thumbnail.altText: An old man sitting in a grey armchair surrounded by his five children, holding his hands.

items.title: Community leaders back Coles supermarket rejection in Kalgoorlie-Boulder

children.content: Welcoming a landmark decision by the consumer watchdog, Kalgoorlie-Boulder's peak business group says the city's 30,000-strong population does not justify the development of another supermarket.

image.altText: A female business executive wearing a suit jacket.

items.title: 'Immense breach of human rights': WA strip searches double in three years

children.content: The number of people strip searched by police officers in Western Australia more than doubles in three years, leading some to label the practice a human rights violation.

image.altText: Exterior of Perth Police Centre, Sept 2024

items.title: Dingo control stopped to protect cultural significance

children.content: Farmers voice concern for their flocks after wild dog control is halted on jointly managed conservation land in parts of the Wheatbelt.

gallery.altText: A dingo glances towards the camera in the late afternoon light.
A tanned dingo stands looking on against the red dirt
Chris is standing looking at the camera with his sheep behind him

thumbnail.altText: A dingo glances towards the camera in the late afternoon light.
A tanned dingo stands looking on against the red dirt
